soundcheck.heard: A power plant in North Pole is holding a ticket, $50,000 just to keep its place in line. The line is a global turbine backlog, jammed by AI data centers, GE-Vernova's backlog near a hundred gigawatts, a fifth of it, data centers, Siemens Energy, over 60% of this year's orders are data centers. Fairbanks lost its cheap power in 2024, its gas deal in 2025. The plant planned ahead, a second turbine, $90 to $120 million, plus a battery bed. But North Pole already fails the federal air test. You cannot add to the pollution in a non-attainment area and be legally correct. The board votes July 28th. Nobody's decided. Not even him.
